item.title: Father of infant boy charged with grievous bodily harm on Sunshine Coast
item.description: A man is charged after his nine-week-old baby was taken to hospital with life-threatening injuries in April.

item.title: Anglican church at Tarraville a marvel of 19th-century timber construction
item.description: The drop slab design of Victoria's oldest timber church is a testament to 19th-century engineering.

item.title: New technology developed in Canberra could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste each year
item.description: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

item.title: Multiple people shot near Ohio festival, US authorities say
item.description: Police are searching for multiple suspects after at least 12 people were injured in a shooting near the Old West End Festival in Toledo, Ohio.

item.title: Family remembers Wilson Sprague as passionate stockman and 'good bloke'
item.description: A pilot and talented stockman, Wilson Sprague's family and friends share their story of the "good bloke", who died in a crash in 2025.

item.title: Boy on bail charged over stabbing of homeless man in Bendigo
item.description: Police have charged a 16-year-old boy who was on bail over the stabbing of a homeless man in Bendigo on Thursday night.

item.title: John Cyril Hawes's canonisation push is progressing. Could he become WA's first saint?
item.description: The life of a world famous architect-turned-Catholic priest is being examined to decide if he should be named Western Australia's first saint. John Cyril Hawes constructed more than a dozen famous church buildings across regional WA.

item.title: Could the AI boom be a bubble?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Choirs bring community together and offer antidote to loneliness
item.description: As winter isolation sets in, community choirs are providing connection, belonging and emotional support.

item.title: How Tasmania's planned new stadium could trigger deal for the Hobart Hurricanes
item.description: Cricket's smallest state says it could become a major player in privatisation, with a potential Hobart Hurricanes deal tied to Tasmania's planned Macquarie Point roofed stadium.

item.title: Greens senator says Australia cannot defend sea trade lanes, rejecting AUKUS reasoning
item.description: Greens defence spokesman David Shoebridge says it is "ridiculous" to think Australia's military could defend the nation's global trade lanes, and acquiring nuclear-powered submarines under AUKUS only invites Australia to a conflict between the United States and China.

item.title: Australia turns its attention to FIFA World Cup after practice games
item.description: The Socceroos have finished their warm-up games, and now turn their attention to Türkiye in their first World Cup match. So, what have we learned from their friendly games against Mexico and Switzerland?

item.title: Israeli troops kill seven-month-old baby in West Bank, Palestinian officials say
item.description: Israeli troops shot at a car in the occupied West Bank, killing a seven-month-old baby and wounding his parents, the Palestinian health ministry says.

item.title: Love story: Accepting my body is my greatest achievement
item.description: I'd take a cure for the pain, but I wouldn't take a cure for my appearance. This is who I am and why should I change myself to make other people more comfortable?

item.title: Longtime local shares lifetime of unique memories from the York Town Hall
item.description: Brian Baker, 95, spent the first 21 years of his life in living quarters at the York Town Hall where his father was caretaker.

item.title: Teenagers, male arrested after machete brawl at Melbourne's Flinders Street Station
item.description: Police say up to 10 people were fighting inside the station on Saturday night before some boarded a train still armed with machetes.

item.title: A 'flamingo revolution' vs a Trump resort. Inside Albania's anti-Kushner protests
item.description: Ivanka Trump has floated "manifestations" of luxury holidays. Meanwhile, Albanians are claiming "irreversible" ecological damage. Why protests are shaking Albania.

item.title: 'Embarrassing' Tigers humiliated by Penrith, Sharks down Dragons
item.description: The Penrith Panthers thrash Wests 68-0 to record the biggest win in club history, as Cronulla notches a 34-12 victory over St George Illawarra at Shark Park.

item.title: Police at Flinders Street Station after a machete brawl
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Homelessness service sees 'concerning' changing demographic in SA clients
item.description: As cost of living and housing pressures intensify, a South Australian homelessness service says more young women and single mothers are reaching out for support.

item.title: Blues beat Dons to make it four in a row, Swans pip Saints in a thriller
item.description: Carlton continues its resurgence under interim coach Josh Fraser with a five-point victory over Essendon, after Sydney completes a thrilling victory over St Kilda.

item.title: Former Channel 4 boss says he 'wouldn't have commissioned' Married at First Sight UK
item.description: Married at First Sight UK could be axed as its broadcaster, Channel 4, awaits an external review into the program and serious rape allegations before "deciding what happens next".

item.title: Gold Coast Suns ruck Max Knobel investigated for alleged homophobic slur in VFL match
item.description: The AFL's integrity unit launches an investigation into fringe Gold Coast ruckman Max Knobel for an alleged homophobic slur during a VFL match.

item.title: SA Police investigating after man dies after 'ineffective' taser arrest
item.description: Police are investigating an incident at Clare, in South Australia's mid north, where a man became unresponsive during an arrest.

item.title: Daniel Turpin named as diver killed in shark attack off Albany, WA
item.description: The family of a 35-year-old diver who died after being bitten by a shark has described him as "an adored husband, son, brother and uncle who had a lifelong love and deep respect for the ocean".

item.title: The legacy of Beethoven's Symphony No. 9 and the composers who came after
item.description: Beethoven's Ninth has topped the ABC Classic 100 countdown for the fifth time. We explore its mystique and shed light on composers following in Beethoven's footsteps.

item.title: Race Around The World recap: From Alaska to India, catch the week one films
item.description: From illegal fishing in the Philippines to Michael Jackson impersonators in Las Vegas, catch up on the films from the very first week of Race Around The World.

item.title: Almost 950 'outstanding' Australians on 2026 King's Birthday Honours list
item.description: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order.

item.title: Richard Scolyer dies aged 59, leaving behind legacy of revolutionary brain cancer research
item.description: The 2024 Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer has died. In his final message he urged all Australians to "pursue their dreams and passion with humility, love and compassion".

item.title: Hundreds of captives in Nigeria freed from militant group Boko Haram
item.description: Hundreds of men, women and children abducted by militant group Boko Haram have been rescued, according to the Nigerian military.

item.title: This week in history quiz, June 8: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?
item.description: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

item.title: Artist Brian Robinson explores everyday magic in Multiverse at Newcastle Art Gallery
item.description: Newcastle Art Gallery presents the distinctive and intricate work of the Torres Strait Islander artist in its first major solo survey following a four-year refurbishment.

item.title: Anthropic sounds the alarm as the AI bubble takes off
item.description: Anthropic wants caution as AI intelligence surges but the stock market can't get enough.

item.title: Federal government accused of AI policy retreat as US tech giants plan Australian investments
item.description: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could lead to harm.

item.title: Parkinson's disease experts warn watchdog as deadline looms on paraquat decision
item.description: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

item.title: Anti-immigration sentiment rises in Japan despite growing demand for foreign workers
item.description: Japan's answer to One Nation skyrockets in popularity after adopting an anti-immigration stance, despite Japan needing foreign workers more than ever.

item.title: Politicians are challenging party lines on migration, gambling and AUKUS
item.description: Former Coalition frontbencher Paul Scarr says inflammatory migration rhetoric is taking a toll on migrant communities. His warning comes as both major parties wrestle with populist pressures.

item.title: When Victoria Police unlawfully disclosed her pregnancy, Joanna Adams took it to VCAT and won
item.description: A woman who says she suffered life-changing stress and anxiety — and later, the death of one of her babies — after a Victoria Police officer unlawfully disclosed that she was pregnant wants her story known.

item.title: Israel retaliates against Iranian missile strikes launched in response to Israeli attack on Beirut
item.description: The Israeli military has fired back at Iran, with state media reporting explosions in three cities after an Iranian strike on northern Israel this morning.

item.title: Denmark's Christian Eriksen collapses during international friendly against Ukraine
item.description: Danish player Christian Eriksen collapses on the pitch in a match against Ukraine but is conscious, the Danish Football Association says in a social media post.

item.title: After Cremer, Willoughby breakthroughs, FOLLO turns to Michael Vrankovic mystery
item.description: A group of volunteers helped find the remains of two long-term missing people in Tasmania this year. Now, they are planning to look for Michael Vrankovic, who has not been seen since January.

item.title: Alexander Zverev defeats Flavio Cobolli in five sets to win men's French Open final
item.description: In his fourth appearance in the final of a major tournament, Alexander Zverev lifts the French Open trophy following a five-set grind against Flavio Cobolli.

item.title: People aren't moving to Tasmania anymore and that has implications for the economy
item.description: From being a hot destination 10 years ago, Tasmania's population growth has ground to a halt. Big changes will be needed to reverse the trend, experts say.

item.title: Swimming legend Clinton 'Super Fish' Stanley honoured with Medal of the Order of Australia
item.description: A 50th birthday and a Medal of the Order of Australia are all in a month's work for Sunshine Coast swimmer Clinton "Super Fish" Stanley.

item.title: Open letter from Richard Scolyer released following death
item.description: Professor Scolyer wrote he "would be delighted and humbled to be remembered as a proud everyday Aussie who 'gave it a crack', and in doing so, inspired others to pursue their dreams and passions with humility, love and compassion".

item.title: Agritourism operators say highland cow encounters bring 'positive impact'
item.description: Traipsing around a farm in the wake of a highland cow or donkey might seem like a quaint rural pastime, but those in the business of agritourism believe there are other benefits.

item.title: Doug Harrison, 97, refuses to slow down after eight decades as a grazier
item.description: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

item.title: Iran weekly briefing: Day 100 of this war marked by escalation between Israel and Iran
item.description: This was day 100 of this war, and it's left us with a tinderbox which could be about to explode, writes our correspondent Matthew Doran.

item.title: Australia crowned rugby sevens world champions after conquering New Zealand in Bordeaux final
item.description: Maddison Levi ignores a knee injury to score a superb double as Australia wraps up the women's rugby sevens World Championship in France.

item.title: Does being in a 'safe seat' influence whether your hospital gets an upgrade?
item.description: Are hospitals in safe seats being overlooked for multi-million-dollar refurbishments by the Victorian government?

item.title: 'It's not normal': Indigenous families question 15 deaths in six months in remote Top End community
item.description: Remote Indigenous communities across Australia are painfully aware that their life expectancy rates are worsening — and in one Northern Territory community, residents are calling for an investigation into a local death rate they feel is "not normal".

item.title: Mount Isa cake decorator stops reporting vandalism because 'nothing gets done'
item.description: After three vandalism incidents in three years, cake decorator Ishani Harney says the emotional and financial toll of crime lingers long after the damage is repaired.

item.title: Iran and Israel halt strikes after Trump warns both sides to 'stop shooting'
item.description: The two countries traded large-scale missile attacks in the most direct fighting between the two warring powers since the implementation of April's ceasefire.

item.title: Rare black-headed gull sighting in Geraldton, WA
item.description: Birdwatchers from across Perth and even interstate have flocked to WA's Midwest after the sighting of a black-headed gull.

item.title: Indigenous siblings pursue careers in medicine, following in the footsteps of their mother
item.description: Ieesha, Abdul and Jamaal Nasser are following in the footsteps of their mother Tatum Maybir, who entered medicine aged 45 after raising her six children.

item.title: Billy Slater to play peacemaker after Maroons stars' fiery club clash
item.description: Queensland coach Billy Slater jokes he might need to table a peace summit between Maroons captain Cameron Munster and fullback Kalyn Ponga as he reveals his team for State of Origin II.

item.title: Critics warn feral pig controls are failing, costing farmers more than $100m
item.description: After an extended dry spell, farmers across inland NSW are reporting significant damage to their paddocks by feral pigs seeking food.

item.title: Charges over 'obscene acts' prompt call for more CCTV in WA's Kimberley
item.description: A woman who reported a terrifying incident while walking at night in Derby says more CCTV cameras would increase safety and help monitor popular locations.

item.title: Anthony Albanese, Julia Gillard join Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan in denouncing sexist billboards
item.description: Premier Jacinta Allan has been joined by Anthony Albanese, Julia Gillard and other politicians — not including Pauline Hanson — in denouncing the language used to describe her on political signs seen in the Melbourne CBD.

item.title: Cyclist seriously injured after crash involving police vehicle at St Helens
item.description: Police say a woman riding a motorised bicycle sustained serious injuries in the crash at St Helens.

item.title: Perth man wants compensation over damage to apartment during murder investigation
item.description: Troy Coward's investment property was the scene of a brutal murder involving his tenants in 2012. More than 13 years later, he is fighting for compensation from police over the impact he says their investigation had on his health and finances.

item.title: Maroons coach explains Reece Walsh selection
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Magnitude-7.8 earthquake in Philippines causes deaths, damage and tsunami
item.description: At least 35 people have been killed in a magnitude-7.8 earthquake that shook the Mindanao region in the southern Philippines, with a tsunami possible on some regional coasts.

item.title: Cancer researcher Richard Scolyer remembered as loving family man who made a difference to patients' lives
item.description: The family of former Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer have paid tribute to his legacy, saying he "wanted cancer patients to know they weren't alone".

item.title: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas has 'open mind' about working with One Nation
item.description: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas says One Nation deserves "respect" and he is open to finding a way of working with the right-wing populist party as support for it grows.

item.title: Titan Ridge volcano in Papua New Guinea inundates Manus coastlines with pumice rocks
item.description: Residents are concerned about access to food and fresh water as pumice makes travel difficult for Papua New Guinea's seafaring communities.

item.title: Mihocek experienced 'lack of feeling' before hospital visit says coach
item.description: Melbourne claims an eight-point win over Collingwood at the MCG as Brody Mihocek is taken to hospital after a serious injury.

item.title: SA government says illicit tobacco prices increasing in response to store crackdown
item.description: South Australia's Consumer and Business Affairs Minister says a price increase for illegal tobacco noticed by inspectors within stores shows the trade is being disrupted.

item.title: Byron Bay bashing leaves Canadian tourist with fractured skull, brain bleed
item.description: A 28-year-old Canadian tourist is in hospital with fractured skull after a brutal assault by strangers in a park at Byron Bay.

item.title: Sunshine Coast father in custody for allegedly critically harming nine-week-old son
item.description: A 30-year-old Queensland man has faced court today, charged with permanently and critically injuring his nine-week-old son.

item.title: NSW town of Wentworth sets vintage tractor record 70 years on from flood
item.description: A New South Wales town hosts a celebration of the "little grey" tractors that helped save it from flooding 70 years ago.

item.title: Nestory Irankunda the face of the Socceroos at FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Nestory Irankunda is set for a breakout moment at the FIFA World Cup as the face of a changing Socceroos team featuring six players of African descent.

item.title: Five tasty ways to use cheap lentils in your meal plan
item.description: From soups to bolognese and oven bakes, lentils are a versatile and affordable meal option.

item.title: Jai Wright's family fight 'never about a sentence' in rare moment of accountability
item.description: Inside a quiet district courtroom in the heart of Sydney’s bustling CBD, a historic moment has unfolded involving a NSW Police sergeant.

item.title: As One Nation nips at his heels the PM has no choice but to play the long game
item.description: The government is playing a long game in responding to the surge of support for One Nation. It has little other choice.

item.title: 'Brazen' Illawarra hammer attack leaves man with fractured skull
item.description: Police on the NSW south coast are investigating an alleged hammer attack that has left a man with a fractured skull and bleeding on the brain.

item.title: Perth paediatrician Chia Liang Saw faces court on alleged child sex charges
item.description: A paediatrician charged with child sex offences may have up to nine victims, with alleged offending captured on "hidden cameras" in consulting rooms, a Perth court hears.

item.title: Blues centre Stephen Crichton ruled out of State of Origin II
item.description: New South Wales have been dealt an injury blow ahead of State of Origin with Stephen Crichton ruled out of the squad due to a shoulder injury.

item.title: Clare mayor express shock at man's death during arrest, after 'ineffective' taser activation
item.description: A local mayor has expressed shock at the death of a man during an arrest at Clare, in South Australia's Mid North on Sunday, as the state's police minister says he is confident the incident will be thoroughly investigated.

item.title: First Big Freeze held at the MCG since the death of AFL legend and MND campaigner Neale Daniher
item.description: The MCG has hosted the first Big Freeze MND fundraiser since the death of AFL legend and motor neurone disease campaigner, Neale Daniher.

item.title: Barunga Festival 2026: Groote Eylandt man nails spear-throwing contest at range of 35 metres
item.description: Sammy Wurrawilya has taken out the title of spear-throwing champion at this year's Barunga Festival with an impressive effort of more than 30 metres.

item.title: Australian dollar dives to two-month low as interest rate fears grip markets
item.description: The Australian dollar falls to its lowest level in over two months, just over 70 cents to the US dollar, amid a renewed surge in the greenback as global interest rates, including US interest rates, rise.

item.title: Four teens in Bendigo police custody following alleged stabbing of homeless man
item.description: Four teens are in police custody following the alleged stabbing of a homeless man in the regional Victorian city of Bendigo last week.

item.title: Police reveal scam script found in Cambodian centre targeting Australians
item.description: Australian and Thai police release a script discovered in a Cambodian scam compound, revealing the high-pressure methods used to coerce Australian victims.

item.title: Israeli Deputy Foreign Minister speaks on the escalating unrest in the Middle East
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: The amazing legacy of Professor Richard Scolyer
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Iran and Israel cease fighting but warn future attacks will trigger retaliation
item.description: Israel and Iran have agreed to hold off attacks but Iran says it will not "retreat from any threat" and Israel warns it too it will "respond with full force" if attacks resume.

item.title: Have you found mould in your rental? Here's what to do next
item.description: Who is responsible for a mould issue depends on a few factors, but experts say it is worth acting early on.

item.title: ABC More or Less quiz: Kings and Queens edition
item.description: The latest ABC More or Less quiz challenges you to rank some of the most famous (and infamous) royals in history, and sort some of the current crop.

item.title: Author Josh Silver channels Yellowface in satirical novel examining privilege and queer identity
item.description: Described as one of the books of the year, Fruit Fly asks who has the right to tell which stories in an age of cancellation for cultural appropriation.

item.title: Australians less satisfied with life than during pandemic as financial pressures mount
item.description: Australians felt a greater sense of satisfaction during the COVID-19 pandemic than they do today, according to government data, when households are grappling with the impact of inflation, rising interest rates and a reduction in real wages.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: From four-time veterans to late bolter Volpato, here's your Socceroos World Cup guide
item.description: Mat Ryan and Mathew Leckie are at a record-equalling fourth FIFA World Cup, while Cristian Volpato headlines a host of first-timers, after his late switch from Italy. Here's your complete guide to the Socceroos' World Cup squad.

item.title: Property and the budget ease pressure on the RBA as two banks cut fixed rates
item.description: With two Australian banks cutting fixed home loan rates, there is a ray of hope that the cycle of interest rate hikes has peaked and borrowing costs may be lower down the track — another sign the pace of economic change has moved into overdrive.

item.title: The most contentious parts of the NDIS bill being scrutinised today
item.description: Politicians are presiding over three days of public hearings into a bill paving the way for the biggest ever cuts to the NDIS.

item.title: US and Iran negotiations in 'deadlock', but ships are finding ways to cross the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Amid new strikes, the future of the Strait of Hormuz has again been plunged into uncertainty, but ships are finding a way through.

item.title: The Taiwan crisis no-one saw coming, and it has nothing to do with war
item.description: Taipei sits at the physical centre of the global AI economy but remains shut out of the diplomatic rooms where its sovereign rights are negotiated, often by others.

item.title: Fire insiders alarmed over data centre expansion in Melbourne's inner west
item.description: Serious concerns about an influx of large-scale data centres in Melbourne's west are being raised by Fire Rescue Victoria insiders.

item.title: Record autumn rain sparks recovery across South Australia, but not all growers benefit
item.description: Twelve months after drought left paddocks bare, record rainfall in parts of South Australia has left some farmers smiling at significant improvements.

item.title: Long delays leave Tasmanians waiting for surgery amid worries about $700m in health cuts
item.description: Patients are waiting years for elective surgery as delays and cancellations mount in Tasmania — can the health system handle the $700 million of cuts still to come?

item.title: Sydney crime figure shot in Vietnam
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sydney's fired-up gangland a new era of 'disorganised crime' as police target powerful violence brokers
item.description: One of the state's top police officers has promised feuding gangs bringing terror to Sydney's streets will not exist "by the time we finish" as police stare down a new era of disorganised crime.

item.title: Hitchhiking wallaby returns to north Qld home after 1,000km trip to Charleville
item.description: Roadkill is a common sight on outback roads, but a group of travellers was dedicated to making sure a joey found in north Queensland survived.

item.title: Family living 'worst nightmare' after purchasing northern suburbs home filled with defects
item.description: After a young family invests their life savings in their dream home, only to discover it is filled with defects, SA's consumer watchdog issues a public warning notice about the builder responsible and seven other traders.

item.title: Rugby league must rise to Kane Evans's level of courage
item.description: Kane Evans showed remarkable bravery to reveal his sexuality to the world and now rugby league must lift to his level.

item.title: England captain Ben Stokes under investigation over nightclub incident following first Test against New Zealand
item.description: Ben Stokes and teammate Gus Atkinson are being investigated by the England and Wales Cricket Board following an early morning nightclub incident.

item.title: Cootamundra community turns out in force for Nate's 10th birthday
item.description: A mother's plea on social media turns into a community-wide extravaganza for her 10-year-old son.

item.title: Christian Eriksen 'doing well' after on-field collapse during Denmark game
item.description: Soccer star Christian Eriksen says he is at home with family and "doing well" after collapsing on the field again while playing for Denmark.

item.title: Coalition 'indistinguishable' from One Nation, PM says — as it happened
item.description: Anthony Albanese says the Coalition is at risk of becoming "indistinguishable" from One Nation after the opposition leader did not rule out a preference deal.

item.title: Bed block patients deteriorate during long waits for aged care beds
item.description: As many medically well patients wait in public hospitals for a spot in aged care, their bodies begin to deteriorate at a rate experts believe is preventable.

item.title: Somali football referee Omar Artan denied entry to US days out from World Cup
item.description: Highly rated Somali referee Omar Artan is turned away from the US just days before he was expected to officiate in the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Australia posts five-wicket win over England in Women's T20 World Cup warm-up match
item.description: Australia cruises to a five-wicket victory over England in its penultimate Women's T20 World Cup warm-up match in Cardiff.

item.title: Will you be in North America for the FIFA World Cup? We want to hear from you
item.description: If you're an Aussie fan making the trip, or already there, share your experience with us.

item.title: People aren't moving to Tasmania anymore
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The criticism Marc Fennell always gets for Stuff the British Stole
item.description: Two years in the making, the Walkley Award-winning journalist is back with the third season of his globe-trotting historical mystery series. He's treating it like it's the last.

item.title: Darwin youth mental health ward found to be unsafe and not fit for purpose, FOI documents reveal
item.description: The Northern Territory's only youth mental health inpatient ward failed to meet 16 national safety standards, documents obtained under Freedom of Information laws have revealed.

item.title: Locusts in Victoria
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu looks to be pushing Donald Trump to his limits
item.description: The US president is in deepening trouble and he does not seem to know how to get himself out of it.

item.title: Inside Sydney's new era of 'disorganised crime'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Why are Poland and Ukraine at odds about their history?
item.description: Poland is weighing whether to strip Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of its top honour for renaming an army unit after Ukrainian nationalist insurgents who massacred Poles in World War II.

item.title: A missing Tasmanian devil has been spotted on CCTV roaming around the Gold Coast.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wildlife carers hone search area as hunt for missing Tasmanian devil enters second week
item.description: A two-year-old Tasmanian devil has been on the loose since last Tuesday when she leapt out of her enclosure at a Gold Coast wildlife park.

item.title: Blues drop Haumole Olakau'atu for State of Origin II, Dylan Lucas to debut against Maroons
item.description: Blues coach Laurie Daley confirms his starting line-up ahead of next week's State of Origin II against Queensland at the MCG.

item.title: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan vows to 'see it through' despite party discontent
item.description: The Victorian premier said she had no plans to step aside despite some Labor MPs becoming increasingly frustrated by her performance as leader and more worrying opinion polls.

item.title: New leader, old problem for WA Liberals as Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation
item.description: As Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation, will the right-wing populist party drag the WA Liberals down again, or will it become a life raft on the way to a political resurrection?

item.title: Doctors say more packaged foods should have health star ratings
item.description: Health bodies want star ratings on all packaged foods to help shoppers make healthier choices, but the grocery industry says the voluntary system works.

item.title: Why I waited until almost 30 to take my first trip overseas
item.description: As I approached the age of 30, I longed to travel outside of Australia, but there was always an excuse or obstacle. A chance to share my culture with the world finally got me there.

item.title: Severe storm leaves homes flooded in South West WA town Harvey
item.description: An unexpected deluge has flooded homes and public facilities in the South West WA town of Harvey. The community was pelted with 40mm of rain in one hour.

item.title: Nauiyu community leaders say residents will return to flood ravaged homes with no services
item.description: Three months ago the town of Nauiyu / Daly River was submerged in the region's worst ever floods. Now, Indigenous leaders worry residents are returning to a community with no services, if the NT and federal government do not step in.

item.title: Idris Elba shuts down idea of a black James Bond
item.description: Idris Elba has quashed rumours he was ever in the running to play James Bond, saying audiences are unlikely to come along for the ride if Bond is played by a black actor.

item.title: US President Donald Trump booed at NBA Finals clash after havoc outside Madison Square Garden
item.description: US President Donald Trump cops the vitriol of the Madison Square Garden crowd after causing chaos to attend the NBA Finals.

item.title: Sitaya Fagan set to make Opals debut against China ahead of FIBA Women's Basketball World Cup
item.description: Teenage sensation Sitaya Fagan is given her first Opals call-up as coach Sandy Brondello turns her focus towards the FIBA Women's Basketball World Cup.

item.title: Brody Mihocek has surgery on neck fracture sustained in Demons' eight-point AFL win over Magpies
item.description: Brody Mihocek is recovering from surgery to fix a neck fracture after he was stretchered off the MCG during the AFL King's Birthday match.

item.title: Police say 17yo fired 30 shots at wrong funeral venue for Sydney crime boss
item.description: Two people are charged over a shooting at what was advertised as the venue for a Sydney crime boss's funeral.

item.title: Medical imaging provider to investigate improper Medicare billing allegations
item.description: One of Australia's largest medical imaging providers, Integral Diagnostics, says it will look into allegations of improper Medicare billing at a regional WA public hospital where the company provides radiology services.

item.title: ABC's Hugh Marks confirms former news director did not know replacement was being sought
item.description: Managing director Hugh Marks is questioned by Sally Sara over Justin Stevens's sudden resignation last month, in addition to controversies over the hiring of Grace Tame and subsequent comments by radio host Charlie Pickering.

item.title: City of Hobart e-bike library program a success and helping to drive sales
item.description: A free e-bike library is set to continue in Hobart after a successful first year and a reported rise in sales, as more people make the switch to e-bikes.

item.title: Crew rescued after US helicopter goes down near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Donald Trump confirms reports of a US Apache helicopter crash near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Fugitive Mongols bikie Brandt Graham arrested in Palmerston after five-day manhunt
item.description: Police have arrested fugitive Top End Mongols bikie Brandt Graham five days after the 44-year-old escaped from custody last week, sparking an "extensive manhunt".

item.title: Former WA Police officer's insurance claim over horrific service injury denied
item.description: Former WA Police officer and WA Australian of the Year Paul Litherland says he is "heartbroken" after his claim for post-service medical compensation over injuries he received in the force was denied.

item.title: NDIS inquiry warned changes could push families into crisis and towards child protection services
item.description: Exhausted families could be plunged further into crisis and have greater interaction with child protection services under the proposed overhaul of the NDIS, advocates say.

item.title: Inspector of Custodial Services releases damning report into conditions at three WA prisons
item.description: WA prisoners are being subjected to "cruel, inhumane or degrading treatment" because the state government has allowed prisons to become "almost fundamentally broken", according to the Inspector of Custodial Services.

item.title: Police upgrade assault charges to murder after death of Darwin man Martin Wiberg
item.description: Police have charged three men who allegedly beat 48-year-old Darwin man Martin Wiberg to death in a "vicious attack" at the Casuarina bus stop earlier this year with his murder.

item.title: Barbeques Galore to close more than 60 stores, with hundreds of workers facing redundancy
item.description: It will shut its 62 company-owned stores in the coming weeks and working through "transitional arrangements" with 27 stores owned by franchises, with hundreds of staff facing redundancy.

item.title: Premier Chris Minns won't face legal action as two charged over campaign donations
item.description: Two men will be prosecuted over donations to Chris Minns's 2015 election campaign, but the NSW premier will not face any legal action himself.

item.title: Aboriginal woman pepper-sprayed by guards during self-harm incident at Canberra's jail
item.description: The use of pepper spray on an Aboriginal woman who was self-harming after being denied access to a cultural arts program was "inappropriate and should not happen," according to the ACT's prison watchdog.

item.title: Acting SA electoral commissioner calls for council elections to be delayed
item.description: South Australia's acting electoral commissioner says she wants council elections delayed until mid-2027 while it awaits a review into the drama-plagued state and Voice elections earlier in the year.

item.title: Cape York figures charged over alleged misuse of Indigenous corporation funds
item.description: Dion Creek, Naomi Hobson and Tim Jaffer are all facing multiple charges relating to the alleged misuse of money from two Aboriginal corporations.

item.title: Iron ore miner Nathan River Resources enters administration owing at least $300 million
item.description: Iron ore mining company Nathan River Resources has gone into voluntary administration owing at least $300 million in debt, unpaid wages and royalties.

item.title: Tasmanian premier apologises for handling of Ogilvie matter, questions remain over his knowledge
item.description: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has apologised and pledged to strengthen transparency around the use of public funds for politicians' legal proceedings, following a minister's resignation.

item.title: Police officers 'acted appropriately' during Clare arrest death incident, SA's top cop says
item.description: SA Police Commissioner Grant Stevens says a preliminary view of the body worn camera vision from an incident at Clare on the weekend where a man died during an arrest shows officers "acted appropriately".

item.title: The ACT government wants 26,000 new homes built in the next five years. Here's how it plans to do that
item.description: The ACT government describes its land release and housing supply program as "ambitious" but "achievable". So what are these 26,000 homes going to look like, and where are they going to be built?

item.title: Di Blessing praises 'hero' husband's bravery during alleged Torrington home invasion
item.description: Stabbing victim Di Blessing praises her husband's bravery during an alleged home invasion in northern New South Wales.

item.title: Foord scores in game 150 as Matildas beat Mexico
item.description: The Matildas earned a 3-1 victory in a frenetic contest against a game Mexican side, as Caitlin Foord scored in her milestone match.

item.title: University of Notre Dame stops taking new nursing students for another year
item.description: The university says it will not take new students for its nursing program for another year, as it needs to "rebalance enrolments across the program within accredited program capacity".

item.title: Man jailed for running over and seriously injuring SA Police officer
item.description: A man, who was on the run from authorities in Victoria, has been jailed for eight years after mowing down a police officer in South Australia.

item.title: Iran says ticket allocation for World Cup withdrawn days before tournament
item.description: Iran's football federation says the 8 per cent quota of tickets allocated to Iran for the FIFA World Cup has been revoked, meaning "there is no possibility to distribute tickets to fans".

item.title: Mongols bikie Brandt Graham flees the Darwin Local Court after being denied bail on Friday. Supplied: NT Police
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Disney on Ice spoiled the sport's finals plans, so how does it secure better venues?
item.description: The 2025 Super Netball Grand Final set a new standard with a record crowd at one of the biggest stadiums in the country. But in 2026, the sport has been forced to retreat to smaller finals venues due to a scheduling issue with Disney on Ice.

item.title: Confidence assured in Macquarie Point Stadium delivery at meeting of AFL presidents
item.description: Despite suggestions that potential delays and cost blowouts of Tasmania's new stadium may be on the minds of AFL heads, the topic was barely mentioned at the presidents' annual meeting.

item.title: Priscilla Hon calls out prize money disparity at Libéma Open tennis tournament
item.description: Priscilla Hon draws attention to the difference between the men's and women's winners' prize money at the Libéma Open in The Netherlands.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Encrypted apps including Signal and Discord used by criminals to lure teens with intellectual disabilities into crime
item.description: Criminal syndicates are targeting vulnerable children with no criminal history, low IQ and intellectual disabilities to carry out their dirty work through encrypted apps.

item.title: Teens recruited by crime syndicates for arson attacks
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Palestinians 'trapped' between Israeli forces, settlers and Hamas, UN report finds
item.description: A UN report finds that Palestinians are being subjected to "mass atrocities" by Israeli forces and being "violently repressed and controlled by the very faction that claims to govern them".

item.title: Tens of thousands displaced after magnitude-7.8 earthquake in Philippines
item.description: Rescuers are combing through collapsed buildings in the southern Philippines after a powerful earthquake devastated coastal communities.

item.title: Sienna Toohey makes Commonwealth Games swimming team after move to Canberra
item.description: Seventeen-year-old Sienna Toohey says qualifying for the Commonwealth Games makes moving away from friends and family to Canberra worth it.

item.title: Opening date for passengers at Western Sydney International Airport announced
item.description: After 15 years of planning, seven years of construction and a year of testing, Western Sydney International Airport has confirmed its opening date for passengers.

item.title: Northern Ireland police appeal for calm after asylum seeker arrested over Belfast stabbing
item.description: Protesters hold anti-immigration demonstrations in Northern Ireland and England after a Sudanese man is arrested over a "brutal" knife attack in Belfast.

item.title: Eight killed in south Lebanon strike as Israel warns entire city to evacuate
item.description: For the first time, Israel's military has warned the entire city of Tyre to evacuate, causing thousands of people to flee.

item.title: Trump vows to respond after Iran shoots down helicopter patrolling Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump accuses Iran of shooting down a US Apache helicopter that was patrolling the Strait of Hormuz, vowing to retaliate.

item.title: Travel quiz, June 3: Stow your luggage in the locker overhead, we're off on a trip
item.description: The lek is the currency of which country? And what's the Japanese word for pufferfish, the sashimi delicacy that's toxic if prepared incorrectly?

item.title: How Rolf Harris hid in plain sight
item.description: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

item.title: Fuel shock spurs business uptake of green equipment including batteries and EVs
item.description: The Iran war and the oil supply squeeze are supercharging business investment in renewables.

item.title: ABC is looking for storytellers for International Day of People with Disability
item.description: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

item.title: Victim-survivors tear up agreement with Catholic church, claiming hypocrisy
item.description: Victim-survivors of clergy abuse say negotiations and agreements reached with the Catholic Diocese of Ballarat are now null and void.

item.title: Australia has a growing homelessness problem. It took one man's death to shine a light on it
item.description: A man dies. His body is not noticed for nearly a week. He was invisible to many, like the growing number of homeless Australia wide.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: When does it start, who are the favourites, what time do the Socceroos play?
item.description: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

item.title: Some countries are protecting a child's right to play — not Australia
item.description: The first time Renae Powell waved her eldest child through the primary school gates she felt something was missing: play time.

item.title: Melbourne councils say state government task force to crack down on illegal dumping is ineffective
item.description: Councils across Melbourne's suburbs are struggling to combat the rise of illegal dumping, despite over $21 million of funding from the Victorian state government.

item.title: National security concerns over use of transcription company VIQ Solutions by 13 government agencies
item.description: Thirteen government agencies are now embroiled in an expanding scandal involving potential data breaches prompting calls for an urgent audit amid concerns over national security.

item.title: New 50 cent airport bus could cost disheartened Townsville business
item.description: An airport shuttle taking passengers through the heart of a regional city will start competing with public transport later this month.

item.title: Palestinian father vows to fight for justice for seven-month-old baby killed by Israeli troops
item.description: A Palestinian father promises to fight for justice for his seven-month-old son, who was shot dead by an Israeli soldier in the West Bank city of Hebron.

item.title: Tasmanian school told to find and exhume graves ahead of planned development over former cemetery
item.description: A Catholic school in northern Tasmania can apply to build new facilities over a former cemetery after a government office declared it "not a cemetery".

item.title: Bus overcrowding prompts push for more Brisbane transit lanes
item.description: Buses are Queensland's most popular form of public transport, but advocates say a lack of investment means they are often unreliable and overcrowded.

item.title: Gary Ablett Jr, John Worsfold among Australian Football Hall of Fame inductees
item.description: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

item.title: Xi Jinping's North Korea visit is unusual, and shows his need to court Kim Jong Un
item.description: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un's bilateral talks in North Korea were a big departure from the Chinese president's usual diplomacy playbook because, for this first time this year, he was the one travelling abroad to make the meeting happen.

item.title: Hitchhiker tells NSW missing persons inquiry of Ivan Milat encounter
item.description: Etched in Steven Clark's memory are the protruding teeth and moustache of the driver he says picked him up as a teenager — a man he now believes was serial killer Ivan Milat.

item.title: Man found dead in a park in Donvale had suffered burns, police say
item.description: Police say the man appeared to have suffered burns and was discovered by a passer-by in parkland in Melbourne's east.

item.title: Kennel owner 'shocked' by condition of dogs received from Greyhound Racing SA
item.description: A dog kennel owner is searching for answers after Greyhound Racing SA put two dogs that appeared malnourished and one with an open wound "you could stick your finger into" in her care. Warning: This article contains images and content that some readers may find distressing.

item.title: Call for security boost at shopping centres to clamp down on knife risk
item.description: A retail worker at Sydney's Broadway Shopping Centre says she was left scared after a man told her he had a knife.

item.title: Australia records first ODI loss to Bangladesh in 21 years as hosts win by 86 runs
item.description: Bangladesh posts just its second victory over Australia in 23 ODIs, recording an 86-run win in the opening match of the series in Dhaka.

item.title: Serena Williams makes winning return to court at Queen's in doubles match
item.description: Serena Williams steals the show at Queen's Club, as the American icon marks her eagerly awaited return to competitive action with a doubles victory nearly four years after her last match.

item.title: Mould, overcrowding and housing delays affecting families in Wilcannia
item.description: Delays in maintenance repairs, including mould removal, are forcing families to sleep on mattresses in living rooms, adding to overcrowding pressures in remote homes.

item.title: Chinese-launched rocket lights up Australian skies as it's bound for orbit
item.description: A Chinese rocket has lit up the skies across Queensland and northern New South Wales overnight, as it passed over north-eastern Australia.

item.title: Live: Tony Abbott says his government was a 'let-down' to many Liberals
item.description: Former prime minister Tony Abbott has told a book launch in Washington that his government was a "let-down" to many Liberals. Follow live.

item.title: NASA unveils Artemis III astronauts to test technology for future moon landing
item.description: NASA has revealed the crew for its Artemis III mission, the next step in the US space agency's plan to eventually land astronauts on the moon.

item.title: Sugarcane industry worries about future as Cairns's urban growth encroaches
item.description: Sugarcane growers near Cairns say urban expansion is reducing farmland and putting the region's sugar industry at risk.

item.title: Live: US military launches strikes on Iran after helicopter downing
item.description: The US has started striking Iran in response to the downing of one of its Apache helicopters, the US Central Command says. Follow live.

item.title: Nick Kyrgios wins opening match in Stuttgart after five-month absence from ATP Tour
item.description: Nick Kyrgios makes a successful return to the court, claiming only his second singles victory since 2022.

item.title: Gippsland croquet player attempts to break world record at 101 years old
item.description: Gippsland's May Beveridge still plays croquet every week. The 101-year-old may soon be crowned the world's oldest female competitive player.
